questions.name: Tool & Software Transparency
questions.score_label: Framework + library + container image named, no full reproducibility stack
questions.evidence: framework='PyTorch'; framework_version='2.0.1'; library_name='transformers'; deployment_info.container_image='example-org/bert-sentiment:1.2.0'. No training-time reproducibility-relevant libs (e.g., transformers version, tokenizers), no Dockerfile reference for training env.
questions.quality_note: Inference container pinned but training environment opaque

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.score_label: Only mentions training in overview, no procedure
questions.evidence: No model_parameters.training_procedure section. Overview says 'Trained on IMDb movie reviews'; version.diff says 'Improved accuracy by 5% through hyperparameter tuning' (no values). No optimizer, LR, batch size, epochs, schedule.
questions.quality_note: Training procedure essentially undocumented

questions.quality_note: Strong version history

questions.evidence: limitations=4 concrete entries (sarcasm 65% accuracy, domain shift, English-only, 1024 token cap); tradeoffs=1 entry (accuracy vs. speed, mentions DistilBERT alternative). NO considerations.out_of_scope_uses.

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: model_index in Papers-with-Code shape; base_model='bert-base-uncased' (HF Hub); dataset_documentation.training_datasets[0].datasheet_url + datasheet_doi='10.18653/v1/P11-1015'; references include arxiv DOI-bearing publication
questions.quality_note: Multiple cross-platform interlinks: PwC, HF base model, D4D datasheet, arxiv

semantic_analysis.format_details: DOI 10.18653/... matches ACL Anthology prefix; datasheet_url present

format_failures.issue: Field absent; training procedure not documented in any structured form
Field absent; no training-time hardware, software stack, GPU-hours, FLOPs, or energy report
No DOI assigned to this fine-tuned model; only an arxiv URL for the underlying BERT paper and a project URL
No runnable code example; inference path is only described in prose plus an endpoint URL

consistency_failures.issue: Stated use cases include 'Content filtering and moderation' but considerations.out_of_scope_uses is empty

consistency_failures.rule: benchmark dataset != training dataset (no train/eval leakage)
consistency_failures.issue: model_index[0].results[0].dataset.type='imdb' matches model_parameters.data[0].name='IMDb Movie Reviews' (the training dataset); accuracy=0.92 reported on the same dataset family used for training

consistency_failures.rule: ethical_considerations mention bias -> bias_model/bias_input/bias_output fields populated
consistency_failures.issue: ethical_considerations[0] discusses dataset bias and version.diff mentions 'Fixed bias in handling negation', yet bias_model/bias_input/bias_output structured fields are empty
No mission_relevance, no funding agency, no grant ID, contributors lack affiliation

plausibility_failures.issue: 92% accuracy on IMDb is plausible for BERT-base, but reporting on the training-data family without a held-out external benchmark (e.g., SST-2 numbers, which is mentioned as an evaluation_dataset but has no metrics) limits credibility
dataset_documentation.evaluation_datasets lists SST-2 ('Used for additional validation') but no SST-2 metrics appear in quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics or model_index

temporal_checks.note: Date is in the past relative to today; bert-base-uncased predates 2025; no temporal inconsistency

documentation_ratio.note: Combined narrative falls in the 50-500 char band -> Q2=3 (not capped lower)

strengths: Strong structural completeness: all required fields, semver+ISO date+meaningful diff, rich tag set, Papers-with-Code-shaped model_index
Apache-2.0 SPDX license cleanly declared and compatible with bert-base-uncased base
Concrete, model-specific limitations and accuracy-vs-speed tradeoff
Cross-platform interlinks: PwC, HF base_model, ACL Anthology DOI for the dataset datasheet, arxiv reference
Full I/O specification including *_format_map structured maps
Performance metrics include multiple slices and a confidence interval on the headline accuracy
weaknesses: Benchmark dataset overlaps with the training dataset (IMDb), undermining the reported 92% accuracy as an external benchmark
No training_procedure: no optimizer, LR, batch size, epochs, or schedule
No compute_infrastructure: no GPU-hours, FLOPs, energy or carbon estimate
No considerations.out_of_scope_uses despite a moderation-adjacent use case
Ethical considerations are present but bias_model / bias_input / bias_output structured fields are empty
No runnable code example in usage_documentation.code_examples
Evaluation dataset SST-2 is listed but has no associated metrics
recommendations: Add training_procedure with optimizer (AdamW), learning rate, batch size, epochs, scheduler, weight decay, and seed
Add compute_infrastructure with hardware, training duration, GPU-hours, and an energy / carbon estimate (e.g., via codecarbon)
Populate considerations.out_of_scope_uses (e.g., 'not for legal, medical, or high-stakes moderation decisions without human review')
Report SST-2 metrics in quantitative_analysis to provide an out-of-distribution-ish benchmark, or replace IMDb metrics with a held-out non-training benchmark
Populate bias_input / bias_model / bias_output structured fields with the demographic and dataset-bias notes already discussed in ethical_considerations
Add a Zenodo or HF Hub DOI for the fine-tuned model itself (currently only the underlying BERT paper has a stable identifier)
Add a funding / mission_relevance / acknowledgement block
Add a runnable usage_documentation.code_examples snippet (transformers pipeline call or curl to the inference endpoint)
